about.heading: About The Body Project
about.body:
The Body Project is committed to providing professional, evidence-based osteopathic care to the Chatswood community and surrounding areas. Our approach combines manual therapy with holistic assessment to address the root causes of pain and dysfunction, rather than simply treating symptoms.

We work with patients of all ages and activity levels, from office workers managing postural strain to athletes recovering from injury. Our goal is to support your body's natural healing processes and help you regain confidence in your movement and everyday activities.

Christian brings years of experience and a genuine commitment to patient care. He takes time to understand your medical history, lifestyle, and goals at every session, ensuring treatment is tailored to your individual needs.

values.title: Evidence-based
values.body: Our treatment is grounded in current research and best practice in osteopathy.

values.title: Holistic approach
values.body: We consider the whole person — body, lifestyle, and health goals — not just isolated symptoms.

values.title: Patient-centred
values.body: Your comfort, progress, and wellbeing are our priority at every step.

service_area.heading: Areas we service
service_area.intro: We're conveniently located in Chatswood, close to the railway station and parking. We also welcome patients from surrounding suburbs.

services.title: Manual Therapy
services.summary: Hands-on osteopathic treatment including joint mobilisation and soft tissue release.
services.intro: Manual therapy is the cornerstone of osteopathic treatment at The Body Project. Christian uses evidence-based hands-on techniques including joint mobilisation and targeted soft tissue release to address the root causes of pain and restricted movement, working holistically to restore mobility and function.
benefits: Improved joint mobility and flexibility
Reduced muscle tension and pain
Better postural alignment and movement patterns

faqs.question: What happens during a manual therapy session?
faqs.answer: Your first session at The Body Project involves a thorough assessment of your posture, movement, and medical history. Christian then uses hands-on techniques tailored to your needs—including joint mobilisation, muscle release, and stretching—to address restrictions and improve function. Sessions typically last 45–60 minutes.

faqs.question: Is manual therapy painful?
faqs.answer: Manual therapy should feel firm but not cause sharp pain. Christian works within your comfort range and communicates throughout treatment. You may feel some muscle soreness for a day or two afterward, similar to exercise, but this is temporary and a sign the body is responding to treatment.

seo.title: Manual Therapy in Chatswood | The Body Project Osteopathy
seo.description: Evidence-based manual therapy and osteopathic treatment in Chatswood. Christian offers hands-on joint mobilisation and soft tissue release to reduce pain and improve mobility.

services.title: Sports Injury Management
services.summary: Professional assessment and treatment of sports-related injuries with return-to-activity support.
services.intro: Whether you've suffered an acute sports injury or have a chronic condition limiting your performance, The Body Project offers evidence-based assessment and treatment to support your recovery. Christian works with athletes to restore function, address biomechanical issues, and get you back to the sport you love safely.
benefits: Early assessment to prevent minor issues becoming major problems
Individualised treatment supporting return to sport
Advice on training modification and injury prevention strategies

faqs.question: How long does it take to recover from a sports injury?
faqs.answer: Recovery time varies depending on the type and severity of injury. At The Body Project, Christian provides an honest assessment after your first session and works with you on a clear pathway back to activity. Some acute injuries improve in 2–4 weeks; chronic issues or complex injuries may take longer. Regular treatment and following advice on rest and activity modification speeds recovery.

faqs.question: Can I still train while being treated?
faqs.answer: That depends on the injury and its severity. Christian assesses your injury and advises on what activity is safe during recovery. Often you can modify training—for example, working around an injured shoulder—while getting treatment. He'll give you clear guidance on what to avoid and how to progress safely.

seo.title: Sports Injury Treatment in Chatswood | The Body Project
seo.description: Sports injury assessment and treatment in Chatswood. Get back to your sport with evidence-based osteopathic care from Christian at The Body Project.

services.title: Postural Assessment & Ergonomics
services.summary: Comprehensive postural evaluation and ergonomic advice to address root causes of pain.
services.intro: Poor posture and suboptimal workspaces are major contributors to chronic pain. At The Body Project, Christian conducts detailed postural assessments and provides ergonomic advice to identify and address the root causes of your discomfort, whether at your desk, in the gym, or in daily life.
benefits: Identify postural habits and movement patterns contributing to pain
Practical ergonomic adjustments for your workspace and daily activities
Targeted exercises and stretches to retrain posture and prevent recurrence

faqs.question: What's the difference between poor posture and postural dysfunction?
faqs.answer: Postural dysfunction goes beyond slouching—it's about how your body moves and holds itself throughout the day. Christian's assessment looks at your alignment, muscle imbalances, and movement patterns to see whether your body is truly balanced. He then shows you what's contributing to pain and how to correct it with specific advice and treatment.

faqs.question: How often will I need postural advice?
faqs.answer: Most people benefit from an initial detailed postural assessment followed by 2–3 follow-up sessions to reinforce corrections and exercise advice. After that, periodic tune-ups help maintain gains. Christian will advise based on your individual situation and goals.

seo.title: Postural Assessment & Ergonomic Advice in Chatswood | The Body Project
seo.description: Postural assessment and ergonomic guidance in Chatswood to identify and address the root causes of pain. Customised advice for desk work, sports, and daily life.

services.title: Workplace Injury Management
services.summary: Treatment and management of work-related injuries with occupational health support.
services.intro: Workplace injuries can significantly impact your ability to work and your quality of life. The Body Project specialises in assessing and treating work-related injuries, helping you return to duties safely. Christian works with you and your employer to develop a management plan that supports your recovery and prevents re-injury.
benefits: Thorough assessment of the injury and contributing workplace factors
Evidence-based treatment to restore function and capacity for work
Liaison with employers and return-to-work planning to support safe recovery

faqs.question: Will my workplace injury be covered by workers compensation?
faqs.answer: Workplace injuries are generally covered by workers compensation, but specific arrangements vary by state and employer. Christian can provide documentation and assessments to support your claim. It's best to notify your employer and workers compensation insurer promptly and get their approval before treatment begins.

faqs.question: How does The Body Project support return to work?
faqs.answer: Christian assesses your capacity and works with you to gradually rebuild function. He may liaise with your employer to identify modified duties or gradual return-to-work plans. His goal is to get you safely back to full duties while preventing re-injury. He'll also give you specific advice on maintaining your recovery once you're back at work.

seo.title: Workplace Injury Treatment in Chatswood | The Body Project
seo.description: Treatment and management of workplace injuries in Chatswood. Christian works with you and your employer to support safe return to work.

faqs.question: What should I expect during my first osteopathic appointment?
faqs.answer: Your first visit (45–60 mins) includes a detailed health history, physical assessment, and treatment. We'll discuss your concerns, goals and any previous injuries. Subsequent appointments are typically 30–45 minutes. Wear comfortable clothing.

seo.title: Osteopath in Neutral Bay | The Body Project

items.question: What is osteopathy?
items.answer: Osteopathy is a hands-on treatment approach that focuses on the relationship between body structures (bones, joints, muscles) and how they function. Osteopaths use manual techniques including mobilisation, manipulation, and soft tissue release to improve mobility, reduce pain, and support your body's natural healing.

items.question: Do I need a referral to see an osteopath?
items.answer: No, you don't need a referral to book an appointment at The Body Project. You can contact us directly to schedule. However, if you're seeking Medicare rebates for certain conditions, your GP may need to provide a referral or management plan.

items.question: Is osteopathy covered by health insurance?
items.answer: Many private health insurance funds in Australia cover osteopathic treatment. We recommend checking with your provider about your coverage before your first appointment. We're happy to provide itemised receipts for insurance claims.

items.question: How long is a typical appointment?
items.answer: Initial appointments usually take 45 to 60 minutes to allow time for a thorough assessment and treatment. Follow-up appointments are typically 30 to 45 minutes. The exact duration depends on your individual needs.

items.question: Is The Body Project AHPRA registered?
items.answer: Yes, our osteopaths are registered with the Australian Health Practitioner Regulation Agency (AHPRA), ensuring they meet strict professional standards and ongoing education requirements.

items.question: What should I bring to my first appointment?
items.answer: Please bring a list of any current medications and previous medical history or imaging (X-rays, scans) if available. Comfortable clothing that allows easy access to the areas being treated is recommended.

seo.title: Osteopathy FAQs | The Body Project Chatswood

about.heading: About The Body Project
about.body:
The Body Project is a professional osteopathic practice dedicated to providing high-quality, evidence-based care to the Chatswood community and surrounding areas.

Our philosophy is simple: effective treatment requires understanding the whole person. We don't just treat isolated symptoms — we assess how your body moves, how your lifestyle affects your health, and what your goals are. This holistic, patient-centred approach allows us to create treatment plans that actually work and last.

Christian, our lead practitioner, brings years of experience and genuine commitment to patient care. He's known for taking time to listen, explain what he finds, and work collaboratively with you toward your health goals. Many patients appreciate his easy-going, professional manner and his willingness to go the extra mile — whether that's answering questions after your appointment or tailoring treatment to your specific needs.

We work with patients of all ages and backgrounds: office workers dealing with postural strain, athletes recovering from injury, parents managing the physical demands of family life, and anyone seeking to improve their mobility and wellbeing. Our goal is to support your body's natural healing processes, help you move more confidently, and give you practical strategies to prevent future problems.

We're conveniently located in Chatswood, close to the railway station and public parking, making it easy to fit your appointment into your busy life.

values.title: Evidence-based practice
values.body: Our treatment methods are grounded in current research and best practices in osteopathy, not tradition or assumption.

values.title: Genuine listening
values.body: We take time to understand your medical history, lifestyle, and what matters to you — and we explain everything clearly.

values.title: Patient partnership
values.body: Your health is a collaboration. We work with you to set realistic goals and give you tools to support your own wellbeing.
